name: Label Studio
tagline: Open-source data labeling for images, text, audio, video, and time series
seoTitle: Label Studio - Open Source Data Labeling Tool
metaDescription: Label Studio is an open-source tool for labeling images, text, audio, video, and time series data. Install with Docker or pip and export to ML model formats.
overview: Label Studio is an open-source data labeling tool from HumanSignal. It gives you a single web UI to annotate many data types - audio, text, images, video, and time series - and to export your labels in formats that machine learning frameworks can read.

It is aimed at ML engineers and data teams who need to prepare raw data or clean up existing training data before training a model. Because the labeling templates and interface are configurable, you can adapt it to custom datasets rather than being locked into a fixed schema.

In the data and labeling space, Label Studio covers the human-in-the-loop side: you can label data by hand, connect machine learning models to pre-label or assist annotation, and integrate it with the rest of your data pipeline.
features: Labels multiple data types in one tool: audio, text, images, video, and time series
Exports annotations to formats that common ML model training pipelines can consume
Included labeling templates so you can start without building a config from scratch
Connect machine learning models to assist labeling and pre-label data
Multiple install paths: Docker, pip, Poetry, and Anaconda
Production-ready Docker Compose stack with Nginx and PostgreSQL, plus an optional MinIO setup for local S3 storage

gettingStarted.intro: Run Label Studio locally with Docker or pip, then open the UI at http://localhost:8080.

steps.heading: Run with Docker
steps.body: Pull the official image and run it, mounting a local folder for your data and labels. The app is served at http://localhost:8080.
steps.code:
docker pull heartexlabs/label-studio:latest
docker run -it -p 8080:8080 -v $(pwd)/mydata:/label-studio/data heartexlabs/label-studio:latest

steps.heading: Or install with pip
steps.body: Label Studio requires Python 3.10 or newer. Install the package, then start the server.
steps.code:
# Requires Python >=3.10
pip install label-studio

# Start the server at http://localhost:8080
label-studio

steps.heading: Open and start labeling
steps.body: Visit http://localhost:8080 in your browser, create a project, pick a labeling template, import your data, and export the annotations when you are done.

useCases: Prepare a raw dataset of images or text for training a new ML model
Clean up and improve existing training data to get more accurate models
Annotate audio, video, or time series data that needs a human-in-the-loop review
Connect a model to pre-label data and have annotators correct it, speeding up labeling
